title: How I Work
subhead: These are a place to start, not a vending machine.

paths.name: Problem Diagnostic
paths.signal: "Something's wrong but we can't name it."
paths.desc: You send me the mess — briefs, decks, research, whatever you have. I read everything, talk to the right people, and come back with what's actually going on. You get a named problem and a proposal for what to do about it.
paths.boundary: This is the diagnosis, not the treatment. Execution and ongoing support are separate conversations.

paths.name: Provocation
paths.signal: "Somewhere along the way, things got stuck" or "everything we do looks like everyone else"
paths.desc: You bring the brief or the stuck point. I spend a day unpacking it and assessing the question underneath it. You get a document of provocation and possible paths forward.
paths.boundary: This is pure thinking applied to existing plans. Not a finished deliverable you can hand to a creative team.

paths.name: Slide Dealer
paths.desc: You have a brief. I produce the artifact — positioning, creative strategy, campaign architecture. You get a finished, usable thing your team can take and build from.
paths.boundary: The brief is the brief. Open-ended exploration and unlimited revision cycles are separate engagements.

paths.name: Embedded Partner
paths.signal: "We need someone who gets us and can wear lots of hats"
paths.desc: Usually starts after a sprint or project — you want me to stick around. We set terms for ongoing strategic access. You get a strategist who carries context across projects and quarters. Outside perspective from someone who knows the inside.
paths.boundary: Not a full-time employee. The outside view only works because I'm not on payroll.
